what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 app-flow composition with three phone screens showing an open, work, and close sequence connected by dashed transport joints.
observations: Uses embedded CSS for the whole composition and imports shared surface variables plus Google fonts.
The layout is a static presentation canvas centered in the browser with a title, three phone mockups, connector joints, a mark, and a footer wash.
The three screens shift from a warm immersive entry state to a cool ledger work state and back to a warm completion state.
The middle screen populates ledger rows and bottom rail icons with a small inline script.
CSS comments explicitly frame joints as transport/navigation and the screens as open, work, and close states.
standouts: The file encodes flow through color temperature: warm entry, cool work, warm closure.
The phone trio makes sequence and transition visible without relying on interaction.
The work screen uses denser ledger rows, metrics, and a single gold action to contrast with the immersive open and confirmation close states.
conventions: Single-file HTML piece with inline styles and inline data population script.
Relies heavily on shared CSS custom properties from /surface/styles/vars.css.
Uses absolute positioning and fixed canvas dimensions rather than responsive layout.
Uses semantic visual labels such as open, work, close, warmth values, and ledger wording.
concerns: The 1600x900 view and 300x600 phone dimensions are fixed, so the composition may not adapt to smaller viewports.
The script expects an external runtime and icon helper to exist; without it, the rail rendering can fail.
There is a no-op assignment in the row rendering callback that has no visible effect.
notes: A static app-flow board showing a choreographed three-step mobile experience through temperature, density, and completion cues.
questions: Is this intended as a presentation artifact only, or should it behave responsively in a browser?
Are the warmth values meant to be data in a larger ledger model or just visual labels for this piece?
Should the bottom rail icons have fallback markup if the external runtime is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents one choreographed app flow as a warm-to-cool-to-warm sequence.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate how an app experience can move through emotional and functional states while preserving a consistent design substrate.
novelty: The distinct element is the explicit treatment of navigation as visible transport joints between three temperature-coded phone states.
contribution: Contributes a concrete flow substrate: staged mobile screens, temperature-coded state changes, ledger-density contrast, connector syntax for transitions, and a completion-state pattern a fused system could keep.
summary_for_embedding: A single-file HTML/CSS app-flow composition showing three connected phone screens for open, ledger work, and close states with warm-cool-warm visual choreography.
intention_for_embedding: This was for expressing a mobile app sequence as a designed flow substrate where color temperature, density, and connectors communicate state movement.
